title: ETH - Insufficient funds for Sending ERC20 tokens, USDT (new1)

actions.value:
To send an Ethereum token, for example USDT, USDC or any other ERC-20 tokens, you'll first need a secondary asset, ETH, in order to pay the gas fees associated with this blockchain or network.

 

With this in mind, your Ethereum tokens may be temporarily stuck, at least until you buy or transfer in around $3-10 of ETH into the same wallet address. This is because erc-20 tokens, themselves, cannot pay these network fees required to move or use these tokens in anyway. We receive no profit from these gas fees.

 


Some Erc-20 tokens can be swapped for ETH, without first having ETH, by taking advantage of the 0x gasless exchange service built into Edge. However, one key exclusion is USDT, so if you're in a situation where you need ETH before being able to send out USDT, you'll first need to either buy some, transfer some in from another source, or have some cryptocurrency that can pay its own fees, (ex. BTC) and use the Exchange tab to swap for some ETH in the 'parent wallet' of your stuck token.

 

For additional perspective, tap into the wallet of your stuck tokens, and then tap Receive. Once you see the QR code, tap on 'Your Wallet Address' below it, and launch the block explorer to view your wallet from your web browser. Here, you can more clearly see the relationship between your 'ETH balance' and the tokens held within this wallet. In Edge, the token assets will have a little symbol of the parent wallet, also having the same wallet name (ex. 'My Ether') and 0x... address for receiving.

 

Please let us know if you have any more questions!
